title: Parafermionic and decoupled multicritical points in a frustrated $\mathbb{Z}_6$ clock chain
abstract: We introduce a generalised six-state clock chain that interpolates between the clock and Potts models via a multicritical point described by decoupled Ising and three-state Potts models. We find that this decoupling extends into stable phases that break only $\mathbb{Z}_2$ or $\mathbb{Z}_3$ symmetry. We also use boundary CFT analysis and level spectroscopy to conclusively identify a $\mathbb{Z}_6$ parafermion multicritical point terminating the clock model Luttinger-liquid phase. Our work shows that parafermions emerge far from integrability, even in systems with intertwined Ising and three-state Potts orders.
summary: This paper investigates a generalized Z6 clock chain to uncover new multicritical points. It demonstrates that Z6 parafermion criticality can emerge in systems far from integrable points through the decoupling of Ising and Potts degrees of freedom.
main_problem: The study aims to identify the universality classes of multicritical points in a generalized Z6 clock chain and determine if Z6 parafermions emerge far from integrable points.
main_result: The authors conclusively identify a Z6 parafermion multicritical point at alpha approx 0.3606 and demonstrate that the model features a decoupled point where Ising and three-state Potts degrees of freedom are independent.
method: Large-scale DMRG simulations, boundary CFT analysis, level spectroscopy, and conformal perturbation theory were used to analyze the phase diagram and scaling dimensions.
model_or_system: A generalized six-state (Z6) clock chain that interpolates between the Z6 clock model and the Z6 Potts model via a tuning parameter alpha.
key_observables: Correlation functions (standard and string), half-chain entanglement entropy (vN), fidelity susceptibility, central charge (c), and deviation from self-duality.
important_parameters: Tuning parameter alpha, coupling ratio J/h, and system size L.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the conformal field theory framework for identifying universality classes and relies on the accuracy of the DMRG truncation error.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the interplay of correlators and entropy under duality; Figure 2 displays behavior near the spin-flop transition; Figure 3 shows the confluence region near the multicritical point; Figure 4/5 illustrate entanglement entropy and the effective Hamiltonian construction.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the generalized Z6 model, explores the phase diagram and decoupling at alpha=1/2, uses DMRG and CFT to identify the Z6 parafermion point, and discusses the implications of parafermionic emergence far from integrability.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) The on-site Hilbert space (in the ˆ𝑍eigenbasis) of the Z6 clock model can be decomposed into an Ising (±, red and blue) and a three-state Potts (numbers 0, 1, 2) degree of freedom. At 𝛼= 1/2, the 𝑋𝑋† terms (grey arrows) cancel in (5); the remaining 𝑋2𝑋−2 (green) and 𝑋3𝑋3 (gold) terms act only on the Potts and Ising degrees of freedom, respectively. (b–d) ⟨𝑋𝑋†⟩, ⟨𝑋2𝑋−2⟩, and ⟨𝑋3𝑋3⟩correlation functions of (5) on a 240-site chain between sites 60 and 181. Red and blue indicate ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic correlations, respectively. (e) Half-chain entanglement entropy of the same ground states. Solid, dashed, and dotted lines indicate first-order, Ising, and BKT...

FIG. 2. Ising correlator ⟨𝑋3 60𝑋3 181⟩(a), dual Ising string correlator ⟨Q180 𝑖=60 𝑍3 𝑖⟩(b), and half-chain entanglement entropy (c) of (5) on a 240-site chain at low 𝐽/ℎnear the spin-flop transition at 𝐽= 0, 𝛼= 2/5 (diamond). Dashed lines indicate the critical lines of the effective transverse-field Ising model (8). (d–f) The same quantities in the confluence region 𝐽≈ℎ, 𝛼≈0.36 (empty circle in Fig. 1). Colour maps match those in Fig. 1. (g) Fidelity susceptibility along the 𝐽= ℎline for 240-site and 600-site chains, indicating two transitions at 𝛼≈0.361, 0.3665. (h) Ising correlator ⟨𝑋3 60𝑋3 181⟩at 𝐽= ℎ(blue dots) with a power-law fit (red).

FIG. 3. DMRG results with open boundaries near the parafermionic multicritical point. (a) Finite-size scaling of the deviation 𝛿𝐸= ⟨ℎ(2)⟩−⟨ℎ(1)⟩from self-duality for open chains at 𝐽= ℎ. (b) 𝛿𝐸and dual spin expectation values as a function of the distance 𝑥from the end of the chain for three values of 𝐿at 𝛼= 0.3606, 𝐽= ℎ, with fits to the relevant Z6 parafermion CFT scaling dimensions.

FIG. 4. DMRG results with periodic boundary conditions at the parafermionic multicritical point 𝛼= 0.3606, 𝐽= ℎ. (a) Entangle- ment entropy 𝑆vN as a function of subsystem size ℓ, with a fit to the CFT prediction 𝑆vN = 𝑐log( ˜ℓ)/3 + const. (b) Lanczos spectrum of the mid-chain DMRG step for 𝐿= 60 (red), compared to the scaling dimensions and multiplicities of the Z6 parafermion CFT (blue).

FIG. 5. (a) Effective Hamiltonian 𝐻𝑐𝑑 𝑎𝑏for estimating the spectrum in Fig. 4(b). (b) Illustration of the conserved Z6 charge on the virtual MPS indices. (c) Evaluating the string operator (11b) in a Z6 sym- metric MPS. Diagonal hatches in the MPS tensors indicate tensors in left or right canonical form. Red diamonds stand for diagonal matrices containing the Schmidt values in mixed canonical form.
